design_philosophy.core_loop: Aim and shoot enemies in first-person perspective while managing weapon power gauge cooldown and protecting allied NPCs
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing aggressive shooting (power gauge depletion) against defensive positioning and trigger discipline
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as a reprogrammed T-800 cyborg protecting John Connor from Skynet's forces across time periods (2029 and 1990s)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict penalty system for human casualties and equipment destruction targets that directly impact ending quality; forces players to balance offensive action with protective responsibility rather than pure firepower

difficulty.lives_system: Health/damage system with stage restart on character death (John Connor killed in stages 3, 6, or 7 incurs large penalty and replay)
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-stage checkpoints; failure to protect John Connor requires replaying that stage from beginning
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Power gauge management creates natural difficulty scaling through trigger discipline; secondary weapon availability and health pickups scattered throughout stages allow skilled play to ease execution burden

progression.upgrade_system: Secondary weapons vary by era: missiles (2029 stages), shotgun shells (1990s stages); health and ammo pickups provide consumable power management
progression.unlock_mechanism: Seven sequential stages with narrative progression: stages 1-4 set in 2029 Skynet war, stages 5-7 set in 1990s protecting John Connor
replayability_hooks: Two possible endings determined by destruction of all Cyberdyne equipment in stage 5
Per-stage bonus scoring for enemy kills, damage inflicted, and casualty avoidance
Stage 3 first-attempt completion bonus incentivizes speedrunning specific segments
John Connor death penalty replay mechanics encourage perfectionistic playthroughs

world_building_techniques: Photorealistic digitized actor sprites from film cast reprising roles
Stage-based narrative progression mirroring film plot: future war → Cyberdyne raid → T-1000 finale
Environmental storytelling through stage themes (battlefield → hideout → truck convoy → facility → mill)
Ending variance based on player destructive choices (Cyberdyne equipment destruction) creates branching narrative consequences

boss_design.philosophy: Thematic final encounter with T-1000 across multiple attack vectors, requiring environmental interaction (liquid nitrogen freezing) rather than pure firepower
notable_bosses: T-1000 in police helicopter (stage 6) — attack pattern includes helicopter pursuit and tanker truck assault
T-1000 liquid nitrogen freezing sequence (stage 7) — requires shooting tanker holes for environmental damage, then knocking frozen form into molten steel
Skynet CPU (stage 4) — facility invasion climax before time displacement

faction_indicators: T-800 cyborg (player) vs. Skynet resistance: reprogrammed machine fighting alongside humans
John Connor as protected objective: represents future human leader worth protecting
Sarah Connor presence: mother figure in resistance narrative
SWAT officers and police as antagonists in 1990s sections: authority figures serving Skynet
danger_signals: T-1000 shape-shifter: technological threat that cannot be permanently defeated until environmental action taken
Hunter-killer aircraft: automated Skynet enforcement in 2029
Terminator endoskeletons: basic foot soldiers representing existential threat
Human casualties from friendly fire: mechanical failure state tied to moral choice
environmental_storytelling: Cyberdyne Systems facility: corporate research site becomes mission-critical destruction target
Steel mill finale location: industrial setting enabling T-1000 permanent destruction via molten metal
2029 war-torn battlefield and hideout progression: shows human resistance infrastructure
Pickup truck convoy stage: civilian protection scenario in hostile territory

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - T2: The Arcade Game (SNES)
